page.content: In the analysis of birth data by region of mother's birth, the UK reports 1,375,306 males born and 1,304,933 females born, yielding a sex ratio of 1.054 and an odds ratio of 1 with no confidence interval or p value available. In contrast, outside the UK, there are 446,166 males and 425,539 females, resulting in a sex ratio of 1.048, an odds ratio of 0.995, and a 95% confidence interval of 0.99 – 1.00 with a p value of 0.035. When broken down by continent, Europe shows 140,445 males and 133,254 females, with a sex ratio of 1.054, odds ratio of 1.00, a confidence interval of 0.99 - 1.01, and a p value of 0.992. Africa has 99,771 males and 96,422 females, a sex ratio of 1.035, odds ratio of 0.98, confidence interval of 0.97 - 0.99, and a p value of <0.001. The Americas and the Caribbean report 29,145 males and 27,435 females, achieving a sex ratio of 1.062, odds ratio of 1.01, a confidence interval of 0.99 - 1.02, with a p value of 0.350.

page.content: Middle East and Asia present 167,146 males and 159,299 females, a sex ratio of 1.049, odds ratio of 1.00, a confidence interval of 0.99 - 1.00, and a p value of 0.231. Lastly, Antarctica and Oceania reports 9,659 males and 9,129 females, a sex ratio of 1.058, odds ratio of 1.00, confidence interval of 0.98 - 1.03, and a p value of 0.790. In the second table, under dose-related variables, for the number of newsletters read (All vs < all) with n = 285, fruit purchase is 2.49 (−241.77, 246.75) g/week, fruit consumption is −0.06 (−0.27, 0.14) servings/day, vegetable purchase is 113.82 (−103.66, 331.31) g/week, vegetable consumption is 0.06 (−0.17, 0.30) servings/day, low-calorie carbonated beverage and water purchase is 188.98 (−502.78, 880.74) mL/week, low-calorie carbonated beverage and water consumption is 0.97** (0.34, 1.60) servings/day, high-calorie carbonated beverage purchase is 507.94 (−407.14, 1423.02) mL/week, and high-calorie carbonated beverage consumption is −0.07 (−0.16, 0.01) servings/day. For used recipes (Yes vs no) with n = 282, fruit purchase is 128.72 (−114.44, 371.88) g/week, fruit consumption is 0.24* (0.03, 0.45) servings/day, vegetable purchase is −13.87 (−233.24, 205.50) g/week, vegetable consumption is 0.27* (0.05, 0.49) servings/day, low-calorie carbonated beverage and water purchase is 248.27 (−317.73, 814.26) mL/week, low-calorie carbonated beverage and water consumption is 0.38 (−0.24, 1.00) servings/day, high-calorie carbonated beverage purchase is 95.23 (−635.92, 826.38) mL/week, and high-calorie carbonated beverage consumption is −0.05 (−0.16, 0.06) servings/day. For accessing price discounts on fruit and vegetables (Yes vs no) with n = 274, fruit purchase is 238.93 (−152.89, 630.75) g/week, fruit consumption is 0.28 (−0.08, 0.63) servings/day, vegetable purchase is 518.86** (158.41, 879.31) g/week, vegetable consumption is 0.48** (0.13, 0.84) servings/day, low-calorie carbonated beverage and water purchase is n/a, low-calorie carbonated beverage and water consumption is n/a, high-calorie carbonated beverage purchase is n/a, and high-calorie carbonated beverage consumption is n/a. Finally, for accessing price discounts on beverages (Yes vs no) with n = 221, fruit purchase is n/a, fruit consumption is n/a, vegetable purchase is n/a, vegetable consumption is n/a, low-calorie carbonated beverage and water purchase is 1004.24* (134.44, 1874.03) mL/week, low-calorie carbonated beverage and water consumption is 0.48 (−0.23, 1.19) servings/day, high-calorie carbonated beverage purchase is 1074.80 (−656.43, 2806.03) mL/week, and high-calorie carbonated beverage consumption is −0.02 (−0.13, 0.08) servings/day.
